metaTitle: Config — Prosperity | Loot Overhaul for Minecraft
metaDescription: Configuration reference for Prosperity. Distance tiers, structure overrides, the container blacklist, indicators, and the prosperity.json file.
ogDescription: Config reference for prosperity.json with distance tiers, structure overrides, and more.
title: Configuration
lede: Every Prosperity setting, organized by section. Tune values by hand or reload them live without a restart.

html: The config file is auto-generated with defaults on first launch at:
Server operators can apply changes without a restart with:
Reloading re-reads the config from disk and syncs updated values to all connected clients.

sections.title: distanceTiers
sections.intro: Distance thresholds, quantity multipliers, and quality boosts for the five loot tiers. Stub — full key reference to follow.

blocks.note: Tier names match those used by loot injection (min_tier) and structure overrides.

sections.title: structureOverrides
sections.intro: Per-structure tier overrides with fixed, minimum, or maximum modes. Stub — see the structure override list in the spec.

html: Example: ocean monuments fixed to Wilderness, strongholds at minimum Outlands, village chests at maximum Frontier.

sections.title: lootTableBlacklist
sections.intro: Opt-in list of loot tables exempt from all Prosperity behavior. Empty by default — every loot container is instanced.

html: Entries are loot-table ids, matched exactly (minecraft:chests/village/village_weaponsmith) or by trailing-* wildcard (somebigmod:* for a whole namespace, minecraft:chests/* for a subtree). Blacklisted containers fall back to vanilla loot behavior — distance scaling, injection, instancing, and the unlooted indicator do not apply.

sections.title: indicators
sections.intro: Client toggle for the unlooted-container sparkle overlay and related feedback.

html: Stub — keys for enabling the overlay, render distance, and the loot-tier action-bar message will be documented here.
